clues.name: Pokédex Habitat
clues.brief: Reveals the habitat category: Grassland, Forest, Cave, Mountain, Sea, WatersEdge, Urban, or RoughTerrain.
clues.tooltip: One of eight habitat categories from the in-game Pokédex. Knowing this rules out large swathes of the Pokédex at once.

clues.name: Can Be Caught in the Wild
clues.brief: Yes/No — does this Pokémon appear as a wild encounter in Gold, Silver, or Crystal?
clues.tooltip: A "No" means it must be obtained another way — evolution, trade, gift, import from RBY, or a special event.

clues.name: Found by Walking
clues.brief: Yes/No — can it be found in tall grass or caves by walking?
clues.tooltip: Does not include Surf, fishing, or headbutting encounters.

clues.name: Found by Surfing
clues.brief: Yes/No — can it be encountered on water while using Surf?
clues.tooltip: Only ~15% of all Pokémon appear this way, so a "Yes" is quite informative.

clues.name: Found by Fishing
clues.brief: Yes/No — can it be caught using the Old, Good, or Super Rod?
clues.tooltip: Only water-type and a few other Pokémon are fishable.

clues.name: Found by Headbutting Trees
clues.brief: Yes/No — can it be found by using Headbutt on trees?
clues.tooltip: About 20% of Pokémon are findable this way. A "Yes" suggests Bug, Normal, or forest-associated types.

clues.name: Obtained from NPC/Trade/Gift
clues.brief: Reveals the specific non-wild obtain method: Starter, Gift, NPC Trade, Fossil, Egg, Import, or none.
clues.tooltip: A specific method can immediately identify the Pokémon or narrow it to a very small group.

clues.name: Number of Family Members
clues.brief: Reveals the total Pokémon in this evolutionary family (1, 2, or 3).
clues.tooltip: A family of 3 means a full unevolved→middle→final chain. Combined with Evolution Stage, this is very effective.

clues.name: Current Evolution Stage
clues.brief: Reveals whether this Pokémon is Unevolved, Middle, Final, or Single-Stage.
clues.tooltip: Eliminates a large portion of the Pokédex immediately.

clues.name: Can Evolve
clues.brief: Yes/No — can this Pokémon evolve into something further?
clues.tooltip: "No" means it's a final-stage or single-stage Pokémon. Cheaper than Current Evolution Stage.

clues.name: Evolves from Another Pokémon
clues.brief: Yes/No — is this Pokémon itself an evolution of something else?
clues.tooltip: Roughly a 50/50 split — one of the most reliably informative yes/no clues.

clues.name: Evolution Method
clues.brief: Reveals how this Pokémon's previous form evolves INTO this one: level-up, item, trade, friendship, time, or none.
clues.tooltip: Important: this describes the evolution METHOD that produces this Pokémon from its pre-evolved form — not how this Pokémon itself evolves further. Especially useful for branching evolutions (e.g. Eevee's evolutions, Tyrogue's evolutions), where the method is a strong identifier since different evolution methods lead to different final forms.

clues.name: Reveal One Weakness
clues.brief: Reveals one weakness not yet shown. Cost increases by +1 per use. Can be purchased up to 6 times (or until exhausted).
clues.tooltip: Each use reveals a different weakness (super-effective type). Cost increases by 1 point with each purchase. Once all weaknesses are revealed the clue shows a terminal message and becomes unavailable.

clues.name: Reveal One Resistance
clues.brief: Reveals one resistance not yet shown. Cost increases by +1 per use. Can be purchased up to 9 times (or until exhausted).
clues.tooltip: Each use reveals a different resistance (reduced-damage type). Cost increases by 1 point with each purchase. Once all resistances are revealed the clue shows a terminal message and becomes unavailable.

clues.name: Has an Immunity
clues.brief: Yes/No — is this Pokémon completely immune to at least one attack type?
clues.tooltip: About 40% of Pokémon have at least one immunity. A "Yes" suggests Ghost, Ground, Normal, Flying, or Electric.

clues.name: Reveal One Type
clues.brief: Reveals one of this Pokémon's types, chosen randomly from its available types (never reveals '—').
clues.tooltip: Randomly picks type 1 or type 2 (if dual-typed). For single-type Pokémon, always reveals that type. Use 'Reveal Second Type' afterward to find the other one.

clues.name: Reveal Second Type
clues.brief: Reveals the OTHER type not shown by 'Reveal One Type'. Requires 'Reveal One Type' first.
clues.tooltip: Only available after purchasing 'Reveal One Type'. Shows the remaining type, or confirms the Pokémon is pure single-type. Combined with the first type reveal, this is often near-uniquely identifying.

clues.name: Base Stat Total Range
clues.brief: Reveals which BST bucket: Under 300 / 300–399 / 400–499 / 500+.
clues.tooltip: Only ~15 Pokémon are in the Under 300 group, making that result very narrowing.

clues.name: Highest Base Stat
clues.brief: Reveals the name of this Pokémon's best stat: HP, Atk, Def, SpA, SpD, or Spe.
clues.tooltip: Tells you the Pokémon's battle archetype — high Atk = physical attacker, high SpD = special wall.

clues.name: Highest Stat with Value
clues.brief: Reveals the best stat name AND its exact number (e.g. 'SpA 130'). Requires 'Highest Base Stat' first.
clues.tooltip: Only available after purchasing 'Highest Base Stat'. Much more specific — the exact number narrows the field heavily.

clues.name: Lowest Base Stat
clues.brief: Reveals the name of this Pokémon's weakest stat.
clues.tooltip: A Pokémon with HP as its lowest stat is unusual; Atk as lowest suggests a special attacker.

clues.name: Lowest Stat with Value
clues.brief: Reveals the weakest stat name AND its exact number (e.g. 'Def 45'). Requires 'Lowest Base Stat' first.
clues.tooltip: Only available after purchasing 'Lowest Base Stat'. Extreme low values like 5 or 10 are very rare and nearly uniquely identifying.

clues.name: Reveal Full Stat Spread
clues.brief: Reveals all six base stats: HP/Atk/Def/SpA/SpD/Spe — a near-unique fingerprint.
clues.tooltip: Almost no two Pokémon share identical stat spreads. The most powerful single clue — use as last resort.

clues.name: Used by Any NPC Trainer
clues.brief: Yes/No — does any NPC trainer in GSC use this Pokémon? (Includes Gym Leaders and Elite Four/Red/Rival, but NOT the Crystal Battle Tower.)
clues.tooltip: Over 85% of Pokémon appear on at least one NPC trainer team — almost always 'Yes'. Note: this includes Gym Leaders and Elite Four/Red/Rival but NOT Crystal Battle Tower (that's a separate clue). Low value but cheap.

clues.name: Used by a Gym Leader
clues.brief: Yes/No — does any Gym Leader (Johto or Kanto) use this Pokémon?
clues.tooltip: About 35% of Pokémon appear on Gym Leader rosters. A 'Yes' means a thematically notable Pokémon. This is a subset of the 'Used by Any NPC Trainer' clue.

clues.name: Used by Elite Four/Red/Rival
clues.brief: Reveals whether this Pokémon is used by the Elite Four, Lance, Red, or the Rival — and which trainer.
clues.tooltip: Only ~25 out of 251 Pokémon qualify. When 'Yes,' the specific trainer name is also revealed, which can be very narrowing. This is a subset of the 'Used by Any NPC Trainer' clue.

clues.name: Used in Crystal Battle Tower
clues.brief: Yes/No — does this Pokémon appear on teams in the Crystal-exclusive Battle Tower? (Separate from regular NPC trainers.)
clues.tooltip: About 30% of Pokémon appear there. Battle Tower is NOT included in the 'Used by Any NPC Trainer' count — it's a separate facility. A 'Yes' suggests a fully-evolved, competitively strong Pokémon.

clues.name: Reveal One Example Moveset
clues.brief: Reveals 4 randomly sampled moves from this Pokémon's full learnset. Cost increases by +1 per use. Unlimited purchases.
clues.tooltip: Each purchase independently samples 4 random moves (moves can repeat between purchases). The combination reflects the Pokémon's type and role. Cost goes up by 1 with each purchase.
clues.example: Surf / Ice Beam / Haze / HP Electric

clues.name: Reveal One TM/HM Move
clues.brief: Reveals a TM/HM move not yet shown. No cost increase. Repeatable until all TM/HMs are exhausted.
clues.tooltip: Each purchase reveals a different TM or HM this Pokémon can learn. Once all have been revealed, the clue becomes unavailable. No cost increase.

clues.name: Reveal One Egg Move
clues.brief: Reveals an egg move not yet shown. Cost increases by +1 per use. Repeatable until all egg moves exhausted.
clues.tooltip: Each purchase reveals a different breeding-only move this Pokémon can learn. Cost increases by 1 per purchase. Once all are revealed, the clue becomes unavailable.

clues.name: Reveal a Competitive Moveset
clues.brief: Reveals a competitive moveset (randomly chosen from unrevealed ones). Up to 4 purchases; fewer if this Pokémon has fewer movesets.
clues.tooltip: Each purchase reveals a different competitive moveset (chosen randomly from those not yet shown). When all are revealed, the next purchase shows a terminal message and the clue becomes unavailable. Near-uniquely identifying.

clues.name: Generation
clues.brief: Reveals whether this is a 1st-generation (Kanto, #1–151) or 2nd-generation (Johto, #152–251) Pokémon.
clues.tooltip: Derived from the National Pokédex number. A broad split — 151 Gen I vs 100 Gen II — so it is cheap, weak, and always available for every Pokémon.

clues.name: First Anime Appearance
clues.brief: Reveals where this Pokémon FIRST appeared in the anime: the season + episode (number and title), or the movie / short / special name.
clues.tooltip: Scope is the original anime before Gen 3: TV Seasons 1–5 (Indigo League through Master Quest), the first four films and their paired Pikachu shorts, plus the live-action Detective Pikachu. Episode debuts use the Japanese EP number + title (not dub season/episode numbering). A couple of Pokémon never appear in scope and will read “does not appear”.
clues.example: S1 (Indigo League) · EP001 — Pokémon: I Choose You!

clues.name: Acquisition / Evolution (Anime)
clues.brief: On-screen events, with the season each happened: “Acquired” (caught, traded for, purchased, hatched, or gifted) and/or “Evolved”. Evolution events are tagged for BOTH forms — the Pokémon shown evolving and the one it becomes.
clues.tooltip: “Acquired” (the C tag) covers any on-screen gain by a main cast member — caught, traded for, purchased, hatched, or gifted. “Evolved” (the E tag) is recorded for BOTH sides of an evolution: the pre-evolution that is shown evolving AND the resulting Pokémon. So a Pokémon that was, say, hatched and later evolved shows both events (e.g. “Acquired S1; Evolved S2”). Only ~55 of 251 Pokémon have any on-screen event; the rest read “No”.
clues.example: Acquired S1 (Indigo League); Evolved S1 (Indigo League)

categories.name: Habitat
categories.desc: Where and how this Pokémon can be found in the wild in Gold, Silver, and Crystal.
categories.tip: Habitat + Evolution Stage early is a cheap and efficient combo that often narrows the field to under 20 Pokémon.

categories.name: Evolution
categories.desc: Information about this Pokémon's evolutionary family and how it evolves.
categories.tip: Family Members + Evolution Stage costs just 6 pts and is extremely effective.

categories.name: Type
categories.desc: Type matchup information — weaknesses, resistances, immunities, and the Pokémon's own typing.
categories.tip: Has an Immunity (2 pts) is a quick filter before committing to the pricier Type reveals.

categories.name: Stats
categories.desc: Base stat information — how powerful this Pokémon is and where its strengths lie.
categories.tip: BST Range + Highest Stat is a great 6-pt combo. Save Full Stat Spread (8 pts) as a last resort.

categories.name: NPC Usage
categories.desc: Whether this Pokémon appears on NPC trainer teams in the games.
categories.tip: Elite Four/Red/Rival (3 pts) is great value if you suspect a powerful final-stage Pokémon.

categories.name: Movesets
categories.desc: Information about the moves this Pokémon can learn and its competitive viability.
categories.tip: Competitive Moveset (6 pts) is the second-most powerful clue. Save it for endgame.

categories.name: Anime
categories.desc: How this Pokémon appears in the original anime — Seasons 1–5 (Indigo League through Master Quest) plus the first four films and paired shorts.
categories.tip: First Anime Appearance is very revealing when it is a movie or short. Acquisition / Evolution narrows hard for the ~55 Pokémon with an on-screen event.

difficulties.name: Easy
difficulties.desc: Generous — every clue category is available and you start with one free clue.

difficulties.name: Normal
difficulties.desc: All categories available. A solid budget, no handouts.

difficulties.name: Medium
difficulties.desc: Habitat and Anime clues are locked out.

difficulties.name: Hard
difficulties.desc: Habitat, Evolution, Trainers and Anime locked. No second-type reveal. Moveset reveals are limited.

difficulties.name: Extreme
difficulties.desc: Same locks as Hard, plus no type reveals at all and no full stat spread. Tightest budget and harshest clue limits.
